activities.name: Chasing segment goals
activities.description: Coach Herve helped me set a new PR for downhill through the canyon. After a few minutes rest we came back up in my fastest time in 3+ years. Felt better than I expected! Then coffee at Herkimer of course.

activities.name: A bit too warm for me
activities.description: Ran with Lauren and Tamara… I cut it short because 60F and sunny doesn’t work too well with this delicate flower

activities.name: Reverse Dayton
activities.description: Nice and cool this morning

activities.name: Trees and water loop with Monica, Melanie, Lauren, Landen, Tamara, and Herve.
activities.description: Fun run… Great group!

activities.name: Little leg stretcher before the beginning of my long camping road trip
activities.description: Hopefully lots of outdoor adventures to follow

activities.name: Palouse Baby!
activities.description: Farm roads of the dry land wheat. Day 2 of my roadtrip

activities.name: The Frenchglen experience
activities.description:
If you find yourself in lonely south eastern Oregon, I can recommend the Frenchglen hotel, which still operates as it must have 100 years ago.

Its on the edge of the Malheur National Wildlife Refuge — a high desert valley watered by runoff from the western slopes of Steen’s mountain.

activities.name: Exploring around the Page Springs campground
activities.description:
👏 1.02 new miles

-- From Wandrer.earth

activities.name: Michael Samples was right
activities.description:
What you need around Steens Mountain is a gravel bike. Steens Mountain loop road is 50+ miles of good gravel and amazing scenery topping out at 9000’. A bit too much for these legs. Photos from my drive on the parts of the road that are open (whole loop by July 1st apparently)　
👏 1.25 new miles

-- From Wandrer.earth

activities.name: Rubicon Trail at Lake Tahoe
activities.description: It claims to be 15 miles round trip and it sure felt like that. Went very slow but the altitude made this a tough one. Stunningly beautiful though!

activities.name: Maggie’s Peak overlooking Lake Tahoe
activities.description: Hiked up, jogged down. Glorious, and trailhead was only 5 minutes from my campsite and a 2nd breakfast to follow. That’s living.

activities.name: Return to Matterhorn Peak
activities.description: About 10 years ago [strava://athletes/4970596/14]David Anderson accommodated my strange desire to find the campsite of Gary Snyder and Jack Kerouac when Gary introduced Jack to the outdoors and climbing in the late 50s. It is described in detail in Kerouac’s “Dharma Bums”. It’s a bus-sized overhanging boulder that Snyder knew about and had camped under before. I returned here, and also found the spot where they apparently sat and meditated while waiting for their friend who was hours behind and didn’t make it up until the next morning.

activities.name: Sneaking up on Half Dome at sunset
activities.description: Many people go to Glacier Point for the sunset view of Half Dome, but if you are on CA 120 I can recommend the very easy hike out to Mt. Watkins which puts you even closer to Half Dome than Glacier Point. It’s totally runnable as long as your lungs are good for running at 8,500’. Mine are not!

activities.name: Modest in Modesto
activities.description: Down by the creek. Paved trail out, unofficial bike trail back. While the clothes were washing at the laundromat. #roadlife

activities.name: Father’s Day hike with Grace and Gilad
activities.description: Matt Davis trail to Steep Ravine to Dipsea. Lovely weather and company!

activities.name: Canyon Creek Trail in the Trinity Alps
activities.description:
With Dave and Ron. Great trail… but damned long. I had dreams of running the smooth downhills, but after 15 miles, nah-ah.

And thank you Strava for adding video support!

activities.name: Rafting on the Trinity with Ron and Dave
activities.description: Yes it was very flat, but we had a few moments of excitement and I swam a couple of times. 100F when we got out of the water!

activities.name: Amazing mountain pool, 1/2 mile from the car
activities.description: With Ron and Dave. Some scrambling was required to get to the this pool which is above the main waterfall that most people visit. Only turned Strava on for the return stroll.

activities.name: Great run with a big crew in the watershed
activities.description: Followed by home made and home grown strawberry rhubarb pie at [strava://athletes/1826934/20]Sergiy Matusevych 🇺🇦 and Natalia’s place. Mmmm good!

activities.name: Streets and canyon with Ana and Herve
Snake by the lake
activities.description:
Sticking to the shady side of the streets　
0.71 new miles

-- From Wandrer.earth
